key_learnings: Structured meal planning reduces food waste by up to 23% and improves diet quality scores. Meal frequency (3 vs. 6 meals/day) has minimal effect on total daily energy expenditure but significantly impacts hunger regulation and adherence. Batch cooking and weekly prep strategies are the most cited behavioral drivers of dietary adherence.
research_findings: Randomized trials show meal planning is associated with healthier food choices, greater dietary variety, and lower BMI. Time-restricted eating windows (8–10 hours) improve metabolic markers independently of caloric restriction. Front-loading calories earlier in the day is associated with better weight management outcomes vs. evening-heavy eating.
current_focus_areas: Building constraint-based meal plan generators that account for allergies, budget, and cooking time; developing circadian-aligned meal scheduling recommendations; integrating with Bots 5, 6, 7, and 9 for cross-module meal optimization.
